input.currentText: [fixture] Library funding usually boosts literacy in mid-size US cities with sustained programs (2015-2020 window): in 12 of 15 cases meeting the inclusion criteria, the effect held; the 3 exceptions had concurrent program changes that confound the comparison. The mechanism is expanded program hours plus more staff for tutoring (per the 2019 ALA white paper). Compared to the no-funding-increase baseline, the literacy gain is ~7% on average.
input.parentText: [fixture] Should the city council approve sustained library funding?
input.threadContextExcerpt: [fixture] Debate room: library funding policy. All-CQs-addressed sanity check fixture.
